seoTitle: Rottnest Island vs Rudds Gully — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Rottnest Island and Rudds Gully. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Rottnest Island vs Rudds Gully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Rottnest Island and Rudds Gully.
heading: Rottnest Island vs Rudds Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Rottnest Island, Western Australia and Rudds Gully, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Rudds Gully has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Rottnest Island (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Rottnest Island or Rudds Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Rottnest Island has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Rudds Gully scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Rottnest Island scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Rudds Gully scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Rottnest Island or Rudds Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Rudds Gully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
